description: GLUTAONE INJ. 1200 mg contains reduced glutathione (GSH) — a tripeptide of glycine, glutamic acid, and cysteine — at a therapeutic 1,200 mg dose formulated for intravenous administration. Glutathione inhibits tyrosinase activity, reducing melanin synthesis and delivering measurable skin brightening, while simultaneously acting as a potent antioxidant that neutralizes reactive oxygen species and supports hepatic detoxification pathways. Indications include hyperpigmentation, melasma, post-inflammatory discolouration, and systemic oxidative load.
protocol: Administer 1,200 mg IV slow push or diluted in 100 mL normal saline over 15–30 minutes, once to twice weekly using a 23–25G butterfly or IV cannula. A standard brightening course is 8–12 sessions; maintenance is monthly thereafter. Monitor for signs of sulphur sensitivity; do not co-administer with oxidising agents in the same line.

description: Glutatine Tab. 100 mg provides oral reduced glutathione (GSH) at 100 mg per tablet, supplied as 100 tablets per bottle for extended supplementation courses. Oral glutathione replenishes systemic GSH pools, supporting antioxidant defences, hepatic phase II detoxification, and gradual reduction of melanin overproduction through tyrosinase inhibition. It is used adjunctively alongside IV glutathione protocols or as a standalone oral brightening and antioxidant maintenance regimen.
protocol: Standard adult dosing is 100–300 mg once daily with meals to improve gastric absorption. For skin brightening protocols, administer alongside ascorbic acid 500–1,000 mg daily to stabilise GSH and enhance bioavailability. Maintenance course typically 3–6 months; reassess clinical response at 8 weeks. Not intended for injection.

description: DERMAGEN Glutathione Film 6X is an ultra-thin dissolvable oral mucosal film engineered for buccal or sublingual glutathione delivery, bypassing first-pass hepatic metabolism to improve bioavailability over standard oral tablets. The film adheres to the oral mucosa and releases reduced glutathione directly into the submucosal capillary network, providing rapid systemic absorption suitable for patients seeking a needle-free GSH supplementation format. Each 15 g sachet contains six application films.
protocol: Place one film on the inner cheek or under the tongue and allow it to dissolve completely without chewing or swallowing; avoid eating or drinking for 5 minutes after application. Use once daily, or as directed by the supervising clinician. For intensive brightening protocols, administer once daily for 4–8 weeks in combination with topical or IV antioxidant therapy.

description: This high-dose ascorbic acid (Vitamin C) injectable solution provides 10 g of L-ascorbic acid per 20 mL vial, formulated for intravenous infusion. Ascorbic acid at pharmacological IV doses acts as a potent antioxidant, stimulates collagen synthesis via hydroxylation of proline and lysine residues, inhibits melanogenesis by reducing dopaquinone, and enhances non-haem iron absorption. Clinical use includes IV brightening drips, immune support, fatigue management, and post-procedure skin recovery.
protocol: Dilute one 20 mL vial (10 g) into 250–500 mL of normal saline or 5% dextrose and infuse IV over 60–90 minutes using a 22–24G cannula, once to twice weekly. For high-dose immune protocols, administer up to 25 g per session under physician supervision with G6PD screening prior to first dose. Typical brightening course: 8–10 sessions.

description: Acogen Inj. delivers ascorbic acid 10 g per 20 mL vial (500 mg/mL) in a sterile, ready-to-dilute injectable format intended for intravenous vitamin C therapy. At pharmacological concentrations, ascorbic acid stimulates fibroblast collagen synthesis, suppresses tyrosinase-mediated melanin production, and provides systemic antioxidant defence against oxidative stress-related ageing. It is indicated for skin brightening protocols, immunomodulation, and adjunctive post-procedure recovery.
protocol: Dilute 20 mL (10 g) into 250–500 mL of normal saline or Ringer's lactate; infuse IV over 60–90 minutes via 22–24G cannula, one to two sessions per week. Screen for G6PD deficiency before initiating high-dose courses. Standard aesthetic course is 8–12 sessions; maintenance one session monthly. Co-administration with glutathione in the same IV line may enhance brightening outcomes.

description: ASCORBIC INJ provides ascorbic acid at 500 mg/mL in a 20 mL multi-dose vial, yielding 10 g per vial for high-concentration IV vitamin C therapy. The formulation supports collagen biosynthesis through prolyl and lysyl hydroxylase co-factor activity, inhibits melanin production by reducing oxidised intermediates in the tyrosinase pathway, and modulates immune response through leukocyte function enhancement. Indicated for IV brightening drips, antioxidant infusions, and wound healing support.
protocol: Dilute in 250–500 mL of 0.9% NaCl and administer IV over 60–90 minutes; typical session frequency is 1–2 times per week using a 22–24G peripheral cannula. Do not administer as undiluted bolus. Perform G6PD testing prior to doses exceeding 10 g. Standard brightening course: 8–12 sessions; long-term maintenance monthly.

description: Ascorbic Acid Injection (Huons) is a pharmaceutical-grade ascorbic acid formulation at 500 mg per 2 mL ampoule, manufactured by Huons (Korea) for IV or IM vitamin C supplementation. Each low-volume ampoule is suited for direct IV push supplementation, mixing into IV drips, or IM use in clinical and aesthetic settings. Ascorbic acid at this concentration supports collagen synthesis, antioxidant defences, and mild skin brightening when administered repeatedly.
protocol: For IV supplementation, add 1–2 ampoules (500–1,000 mg) to an existing IV drip bag or administer slow IV push over 2–5 minutes diluted in 10–20 mL saline. For IM use, inject 500 mg into the gluteal or deltoid muscle using a 23G 25 mm needle. Administer 2–3 times weekly for maintenance or adjunctive brightening protocols.

description: ATP-S Inj. delivers adenosine triphosphate (ATP) at 10.98 mg/mL in 2 mL ampoules, providing exogenous ATP for cellular energy metabolism and peripheral vasodilation. Exogenous ATP stimulates purinergic P1 and P2 receptors, facilitating vasodilation, improving microcirculatory perfusion, and supporting cellular energy turnover in tissues with increased metabolic demand. It is used in aesthetic and wellness IV protocols targeting fatigue, poor circulation, and skin vitality, as well as in cardiac electrophysiology diagnostics.
protocol: For aesthetic IV wellness protocols, dilute 1–2 ampoules in 100–250 mL of normal saline and infuse slowly over 30–60 minutes via 23–25G cannula, typically once to twice weekly. Rate must not exceed 10 mg/minute to avoid transient palpitations or flushing. Use under clinical supervision; contraindicated in patients with AV block or bronchospasm.

description: Acepain Inj. is a ready-to-use intravenous solution of acetaminophen (paracetamol) at 1 g per 100 mL, supplied as a pre-filled IV infusion bag for perioperative and post-procedural analgesia. Acetaminophen inhibits central prostaglandin synthesis via cyclooxygenase pathways in the CNS, providing non-opioid pain relief with a rapid onset of approximately 5 minutes after IV administration and duration of up to 4 hours per dose. It is indicated for moderate post-procedural pain management in aesthetic and surgical settings.
protocol: Infuse the full 100 mL bag IV over 15 minutes using a standard IV administration set; no dilution required. Adult dose: 1 g every 6 hours (maximum 4 g/day). Use as part of a multimodal analgesia protocol post-procedure. Reduce dose or frequency in patients with hepatic impairment or body weight under 50 kg. Monitor liver function with repeated courses.

description: Multivita is a lyophilised multivitamin preparation combining fat-soluble vitamins (A, D2, E) and water-soluble B-complex vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B5, B6, B12, biotin, folic acid) with ascorbic acid, designed for IV parenteral supplementation or adjunctive vitamin infusion therapy. Lyophilisation preserves the potency of heat-labile vitamins in a sterile powder form that requires reconstitution before administration. Indicated for vitamin deficiency states, peri-operative nutritional support, and aesthetic IV drip protocols.
protocol: Reconstitute one lyophilised vial with 5 mL sterile water for injection by gentle swirling; do not shake vigorously. Within 5 minutes of reconstitution, transfer the solution into 250–500 mL of isotonic saline or 5% dextrose and infuse IV over 60–90 minutes. Administer once daily or as clinically directed; inspect reconstituted solution for clarity and discolouration before use.

description: Vita-D (200,000 IU) is a high-dose cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3) injectable solution at 5 mg/mL (200,000 IU per 1 mL ampoule), formulated for intramuscular administration as a single-dose correction of vitamin D deficiency. Cholecalciferol is converted in the liver to 25-hydroxyvitamin D and subsequently to the active 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D3 in the kidneys, regulating calcium and phosphate homeostasis, supporting immune modulation, and influencing skin cell differentiation and proliferation. Indicated for correction of clinical or subclinical vitamin D deficiency.
protocol: Administer 200,000 IU (1 mL) by deep intramuscular injection into the gluteus maximus or vastus lateralis using a 23G 38 mm needle; aspirate before injection. A single loading dose is given for confirmed deficiency; repeat at 12 weeks if serum 25(OH)D remains below 30 ng/mL. Monitor serum calcium and 25(OH)D at 12 and 24 weeks. Avoid concurrent high-dose oral vitamin D supplementation.

description: Vita-D (300,000 IU) provides cholecalciferol at a 300,000 IU dose per 1.5 mL ampoule, representing a higher single-dose depot injection for rapid correction of severe vitamin D deficiency. The oil-based or aqueous cholecalciferol depot releases gradually from the intramuscular injection site, sustaining elevation of serum 25(OH)D levels over weeks to months following a single administration. Indicated for patients with severe deficiency (serum 25[OH]D < 20 ng/mL) or malabsorption syndromes where oral supplementation is unreliable.
protocol: Administer 300,000 IU (1.5 mL) by deep intramuscular injection into the gluteus maximus using a 23G 38 mm needle with aspiration; a single loading dose is typical for severe deficiency. Reassess serum 25(OH)D at 12 weeks; if levels remain below 30 ng/mL, consider a second dose. Monitor serum calcium and renal function; use caution in patients with granulomatous conditions or primary hyperparathyroidism.

description: Vita D-bone Inj. delivers cholecalciferol at 200,000 IU per dose in a sterile injectable form designed for intramuscular administration to support bone mineralisation and correct vitamin D deficiency. Cholecalciferol undergoes hepatic hydroxylation to 25(OH)D3 and renal activation to calcitriol, the principal hormonal regulator of intestinal calcium absorption and bone remodelling. The formulation is indicated for vitamin D deficiency-related osteopenia, osteoporosis risk reduction, and adjunctive immune support.
protocol: Administer 200,000 IU by deep IM injection into the gluteus maximus or vastus lateralis using a 23G needle; aspirate before injection to confirm extravascular placement. For deficiency correction, administer a single dose and reassess serum 25(OH)D at 12 weeks. Maintenance doses may be repeated every 3–6 months based on serum levels. Co-administer calcium supplementation as clinically indicated.

description: BM Hid Inj. provides cholecalciferol (Vitamin D3) at 200,000 IU per 1 mL vial, formulated as a sterile injectable solution for intramuscular administration in the correction of vitamin D deficiency. Cholecalciferol is the most physiologically relevant oral and injectable form of vitamin D, producing sustained elevations in serum 25(OH)D when administered as a high-dose IM depot, with clinical evidence supporting efficacy in Korean and other Asian populations with prevalent deficiency. Indicated for deficiency correction, immune modulation, and bone health support.
protocol: Administer 200,000 IU (1 mL) by deep intramuscular injection into the gluteus maximus or vastus lateralis using a 23G 38 mm needle; aspirate to confirm placement. Administer a single loading dose for confirmed deficiency; repeat at 12 weeks if 25(OH)D remains subtherapeutic. Monitor serum 25(OH)D and corrected serum calcium at 12-week intervals. Avoid in hypercalcaemia or hypervitaminosis D.

description: Doxy Tab. (300) provides doxycycline hyclate 100 mg oral tablets in a 300-tablet professional pack for extended antibiotic and anti-inflammatory courses. Doxycycline is a broad-spectrum tetracycline antibiotic that inhibits bacterial protein synthesis by reversibly binding the 30S ribosomal subunit; at sub-antimicrobial doses it additionally suppresses matrix metalloproteinases and inflammatory cytokines, underpinning its use in inflammatory acne, rosacea, and post-procedure prophylaxis. The 300-tablet pack is sized for institutional or high-volume clinical use.
protocol: For moderate-to-severe acne or rosacea: administer 100 mg orally once or twice daily with food and a full glass of water to reduce oesophageal irritation. Standard antibiotic course: 8–12 weeks; at sub-antimicrobial dosing (40 mg modified-release), continuous use is appropriate for rosacea maintenance. Avoid concomitant antacids, dairy, or iron within 2 hours of dosing. Counsel patients on photosensitivity risk.

description: Doxy Tab. (30) provides doxycycline hyclate 100 mg oral tablets in a 30-tablet pack suitable for standard antibiotic courses in acne, rosacea, or peri-procedural prophylaxis. Doxycycline inhibits bacterial 30S ribosomal translation and exerts anti-inflammatory effects through MMP inhibition and cytokine suppression, making it effective for both infection control and inflammatory skin conditions. The 30-tablet format corresponds to a standard 4-week treatment course at 100 mg once daily.
protocol: Administer 100 mg orally once daily (or 100 mg twice daily for more severe presentations) with food and a full glass of water; avoid lying down for 30 minutes post-dose. Standard course: 4 weeks at this pack size. Co-prescribe a topical retinoid or benzoyl peroxide for acne synergy. Contraindicated in pregnancy and children under 12; monitor for GI intolerance, photosensitivity, and yeast overgrowth.

description: MESOCARTIN INJ is an L-carnitine-based injectable solution formulated for intramuscular or intravenous administration to support fatty acid beta-oxidation and energy metabolism. L-carnitine is an essential quaternary ammonium compound that transports long-chain fatty acids across the inner mitochondrial membrane, enabling their oxidative conversion to ATP, which drives fat mobilisation, reduces fatigue, and supports metabolic body contouring protocols. Each box contains 10 vials for structured lipolytic or energy-support treatment courses.
protocol: For aesthetic fat-reduction protocols, administer 1–2 vials (2,000 mg) by IM injection into the gluteus maximus or by slow IV push/infusion diluted in 100 mL saline, once or twice weekly at minimum 5-day intervals. A standard course is 8–12 sessions. Combine with mesotherapy lipolytic techniques for localised fat areas. Monitor for mild GI symptoms; generally well tolerated.

description: LIPOTOCIN INJ contains thioctic acid (alpha-lipoic acid, ALA) at 300 mg per 12 mL ampoule, providing a high-potency injectable antioxidant for IV infusion therapy. Alpha-lipoic acid is a mitochondrial co-enzyme that both regenerates endogenous antioxidants (glutathione, vitamins C and E) and directly scavenges reactive oxygen species, with additional roles in glucose metabolism, insulin sensitisation, and inhibition of NF-κB-mediated inflammatory pathways. Indicated for diabetic polyneuropathy, oxidative-stress driven skin conditions (melasma, photo-ageing, acne), and metabolic support.
protocol: Dilute one ampoule (300 mg/12 mL) in 100–250 mL of normal saline and infuse IV over at least 30 minutes via 22–25G cannula, at a maximum rate of 50 mg/minute to avoid adverse reactions. Administer once daily for severe neuropathy or 2–3 times weekly for aesthetic protocols. A standard course is 10–20 sessions; sensitive patients should be titrated starting from a lower dose. Protect diluted solution from light.

description: HYCOBAL INJ. provides hydroxocobalamin (Vitamin B12a) at 5 mg per 2 mL ampoule for intramuscular or intravenous administration as a high-potency B12 repletion therapy. Hydroxocobalamin is the preferred depot form of cobalamin, retained in tissues significantly longer than cyanocobalamin; it acts as a co-factor for methionine synthase and methylmalonyl-CoA mutase, supporting DNA synthesis, myelin maintenance, neurological function, and erythropoiesis. Indicated for B12 deficiency, pernicious anaemia, peripheral neuropathy, and adjunctive neurological and energy support infusions.
protocol: For B12 deficiency repletion: administer 1,000–5,000 mcg (0.4–2 mL) IM into the gluteus maximus or deltoid 3 times weekly for 2–4 weeks using a 23G 25 mm needle, then monthly for maintenance. For IV wellness protocols, add to an existing drip at 1,000–5,000 mcg per session. Rotate injection sites to avoid local induration; monitor serum B12, CBC, and methylmalonic acid response.

description: B-COLAMIN VIT B12 delivers cyanocobalamin or hydroxocobalamin as a 2 mL injectable solution per ampoule, supplied in a 50-ampoule box for high-volume clinical B12 supplementation. Cobalamin is essential for the conversion of homocysteine to methionine and methylmalonyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA, supporting neurological integrity, erythrocyte maturation, and energy metabolism. Indicated for vitamin B12 deficiency, pernicious anaemia, fatigue states, and adjunctive IV wellness and neurological support protocols.
protocol: Administer 1 ampoule (2 mL) intramuscularly into the gluteus or deltoid using a 23G 25 mm needle, 2–3 times weekly during loading phase, then once weekly or monthly for maintenance. For IV aesthetic protocols, add to an IV drip bag or administer slow IV push over 1–2 minutes. Monitor serum B12 and CBC at baseline and 3 months to assess response.

description: BeeCom Hexa Vitamin B Complex delivers a complete spectrum of B vitamins — B1 (thiamine), B2 (riboflavin), B3 (niacinamide), B5 (dexpanthenol), B6 (pyridoxine), B7 (biotin), and B12 (cyanocobalamin) — in a 2 mL injectable ampoule for IM or IV administration. This hexa-B formulation supports energy metabolism (ATP synthesis via the TCA cycle), neurological function (myelin synthesis, neurotransmitter production), erythropoiesis, and skin cell turnover — making it widely used in aesthetic IV drip cocktails for skin radiance, fatigue, and hair health. Supplied as 50 ampoules per box.
protocol: Administer 1 ampoule (2 mL) IM into the gluteus or deltoid using a 23–25G needle, or add to an IV drip bag for slow infusion over 30–60 minutes, 2–3 times weekly. For intensive aesthetic revitalisation protocols, combine with Vitamin C and glutathione infusions. Maintenance dosing is one to two injections per week. Caution: high-dose B6 (pyridoxine) over extended periods may cause sensory neuropathy.

description: ZINC INJ is a zinc sulfate hydrate sterile injectable solution delivered in 10 mL vials, providing parenteral zinc supplementation for IV or IM administration. Zinc is an essential trace element that serves as a catalytic co-factor for over 300 enzymes, plays a structural role in zinc finger transcription factors, and is critical for immune function, wound healing, keratinocyte proliferation, sebaceous gland regulation, and collagen cross-linking. Indicated for zinc deficiency, acne management, immunodeficiency support, scar regeneration, and hair loss protocols.
protocol: For IV trace element supplementation: add the required volume of zinc sulfate solution to a parenteral nutrition bag or 250 mL saline and infuse over 60–90 minutes via 22–25G cannula. Typical IV aesthetic dose: 1–3 mg elemental zinc per session, 1–2 times weekly. For acne and skin protocols, combine with vitamin C and B-complex infusions. Monitor serum zinc levels periodically; excess zinc can interfere with copper absorption.

description: Zinc S Inj. provides zinc sulfate hydrate in a 5 mL sterile injectable vial for parenteral zinc supplementation, offering a smaller-format option suited to individual session dosing or supplementation of IV cocktails. Zinc acts as a structural component of metalloenzymes, supports T-cell and NK cell immune activity, and in dermatology is used for its antioxidant, anti-seborrhoeic, and wound-healing properties. It is indicated for zinc deficiency states, acne vulgaris, hair thinning, and adjunctive IV wellness protocols.
protocol: Add the required elemental zinc dose from the 5 mL vial to an IV drip (250–500 mL normal saline) and infuse over 60 minutes via 22–25G cannula, or administer via IM injection into the deltoid or gluteus using a 23G needle. Typical aesthetic IV dose: 1–3 mg elemental zinc per session, one to two sessions weekly. Monitor serum zinc and copper; long-term zinc supplementation may deplete copper.

description: MAXYBLUE INJ is a sterile multi-trace mineral injectable solution supplying five essential microelements — zinc (Zn), copper (Cu), manganese (Mn), selenium (Se), and chromium (Cr) — in a single 10 mL vial for IV administration. Each element fulfils specific enzymatic roles: zinc supports wound healing and immune function; copper is a co-factor for ceruloplasmin and lysyl oxidase; manganese activates mitochondrial superoxide dismutase; selenium is integral to glutathione peroxidase activity; and chromium enhances insulin receptor sensitivity. Indicated for trace element repletion in patients on IV nutrition, immune support protocols, and metabolic wellness programmes.
protocol: Add one 10 mL vial to an appropriate parenteral nutrition admixture or 250–500 mL saline and infuse IV over 60–90 minutes via 22–25G cannula, once daily for repletion or 2–3 times weekly for maintenance supplementation. Do not administer undiluted. Monitor serum trace element levels at baseline and periodically. Avoid concurrent high-dose zinc supplementation from separate sources to prevent copper displacement.

description: Hishiphagen-C Inj. is a Japanese-origin compound injectable containing monoammonium glycyrrhizinate 53 mg (40 mg as glycyrrhizic acid), glycine 400 mg, and L-cysteine 20 mg per 20 mL ampoule. Glycyrrhizinate (derived from liquorice root) exerts hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects through glucocorticoid metabolism modulation and NF-κB pathway suppression; L-cysteine and glycine provide antioxidant support and serve as glutathione precursors for hepatic detoxification. Indicated for chronic hepatitis, drug-induced hepatotoxicity, allergic dermatoses, eczema, urticaria, and general fatigue recovery.
protocol: Administer 20–40 mL IV (slow IV push or diluted in 100–250 mL saline) once daily; for chronic hepatic disease, dose may be increased to 40–60 mL IV daily under physician supervision, not exceeding 100 mL/day. For general detox and skin protocols, administer 20 mL 2–3 times weekly using a 23–25G cannula. Monitor blood pressure (pseudoaldosteronism risk with prolonged use) and serum potassium.

description: CARTIN Inj. provides L-carnitine at 1,000 mg per 10 mL ampoule for intravenous or intramuscular administration, supporting mitochondrial fatty acid oxidation and energy production. L-carnitine facilitates the translocation of long-chain acylcarnitine esters across the inner mitochondrial membrane, making it essential for beta-oxidation of stored fat; injectable delivery bypasses gastrointestinal absorption limitations to achieve rapid plasma concentrations useful in lipolytic and metabolic enhancement protocols. Indicated for carnitine deficiency, fat-reduction IV protocols, fatigue, and metabolic support.
protocol: Administer 1,000 mg (10 mL) by slow IV infusion diluted in 100 mL saline over 30–60 minutes, or by IM injection into the gluteus maximus using a 23G 38 mm needle, once or twice weekly. For body contouring drip protocols, combine with alpha-lipoic acid and B-complex in the same infusion. Standard course: 8–12 sessions. Rotate IM injection sites; IV route preferred for rapid absorption.

description: Chioctocin Inj. is an injectable formulation of thioctic acid (alpha-lipoic acid) at 25 mg per 5 mL ampoule, providing a lower-concentration IV-compatible antioxidant dose for gentle metabolic and neuroprotective support. Alpha-lipoic acid is a universal antioxidant active in both aqueous and lipid cellular compartments, regenerating endogenous antioxidants including glutathione, vitamins C and E, and coenzyme Q10, while also inhibiting advanced glycation end-product formation and supporting peripheral nerve function. Indicated for mild diabetic or chemotherapy-induced neuropathy, antioxidant IV cocktails, and metabolic support protocols.
protocol: Dilute one ampoule (25 mg/5 mL) in 100–250 mL of normal saline and infuse IV over 30 minutes via 23–25G cannula, protecting from direct light. For neuropathy protocols, administer once daily (up to 600 mg total daily dose across multiple ampoules) for 2–4 weeks, then transition to oral supplementation at 600 mg/day. For aesthetic antioxidant drips, use 25–75 mg as an additive to multivitamin infusions, 1–2 times weekly.

description: WELLSCAINE PLUS CREAM is a eutectic mixture of lidocaine 25 mg/g and prilocaine 25 mg/g (EMLA-type formulation) in a 500 g professional bulk tub for pre-procedural topical anaesthesia. The eutectic combination lowers the melting point of both anaesthetics, enabling effective dermal penetration as a cream, with lidocaine and prilocaine blocking voltage-gated sodium channels in peripheral nerve fibres to produce dose-dependent cutaneous anaesthesia up to 5 mm depth under occlusion. Indicated for needle injections, laser treatments, mesotherapy, microneedling, thread lifting, and permanent cosmetics.
protocol: Cleanse the treatment area; apply a 2–3 mm thick layer and cover with occlusive dressing for 30–45 minutes (45–60 minutes for deeper procedures such as thread lifting at 5–8 mm). Remove cream and wipe area before proceeding. Maximum dose per session: 184 mg lidocaine (approximately 7.4 g cream) over intact skin. Do not apply to mucosae or broken skin without specialist guidance. Effect duration: approximately 60–90 minutes after removal.

description: LIDCAIN CREAM is a high-concentration topical anaesthetic formulated at 10.56% lidocaine, supplied in a 500 g professional bulk tub for large-volume or multi-patient clinical use. Lidocaine reversibly blocks voltage-gated sodium channels in peripheral sensory nerve endings, temporarily interrupting pain signal transmission from the application site; the 10.56% concentration represents the highest commercially available topical lidocaine level, enabling effective surface analgesia for minimally invasive cosmetic procedures without systemic absorption at recommended doses. Indicated for laser resurfacing, microneedling, semi-permanent make-up, filler injection, and hair removal.
protocol: Apply a thick uniform layer (approximately 1–2 g/10 cm²) to the clean, dry treatment area and cover with occlusive film for 15–25 minutes; remove immediately before the procedure and wipe the area clean. Maximum dose: 1.74 g cream (187 mg lidocaine) per 24 hours on intact skin. Do not apply to mucous membranes or damaged skin. Anaesthetic effect lasts 60–90 minutes post-removal.

description: WELLSCAINE CREAM is a 9.6% lidocaine-only topical anaesthetic supplied in a 500 g professional tub, formulated for pre-procedural surface numbing across a broad range of aesthetic and minor surgical applications. The 9.6% lidocaine base provides reliable dermal anaesthesia via sodium channel blockade in cutaneous nociceptors, with an onset of 15–20 minutes under occlusion and a depth of anaesthesia suitable for superficial-to-mid-dermal procedures. Indicated for injections, laser treatments, fractional resurfacing, mesotherapy, and semi-permanent cosmetics.
protocol: Apply a 2–3 mm layer to the clean treatment area, cover with plastic occlusive wrap, and leave for 20–30 minutes; remove excess cream with a gauze pad before the procedure. Maximum dose: do not exceed 187 mg lidocaine per 24 hours on intact skin. Avoid open wounds and mucosal surfaces. Anaesthetic effect persists approximately 60–90 minutes after application; proceed within this window.

description: BERACAINE SPRAY is a fast-acting topical anaesthetic containing lidocaine 10% in a metered-dose 50 mL spray bottle, designed for rapid surface numbing prior to minimally invasive aesthetic and dermatological procedures. The spray format delivers a fine, even mist of lidocaine that diffuses across the stratum corneum within 1–2 minutes, blocking peripheral sodium channels to achieve mucosal or surface analgesia suitable for injections, tattooing, piercing, laser ablation, and permanent cosmetics. The metered delivery enables precise dose control without over-application.
protocol: Hold the spray bottle 5–10 cm from the skin surface and spray 1–3 short bursts (approximately 10 mg lidocaine per spray) onto the clean treatment area; allow 1–2 minutes for onset before proceeding. For mucosal use (dentistry, otolaryngology), apply no more than 2–3 sprays per session. Maximum recommended dose: 200 mg lidocaine per 24 hours. Do not spray near eyes or onto broken skin. Anaesthetic effect lasts 60–90 minutes.

description: NEO-CAIN CREAM (30 g tube) is a high-concentration topical surface anaesthetic formulated at 10.56% lidocaine in a convenient 30 g single-patient tube format. Lidocaine blocks sodium ion influx through voltage-gated channels in sensory neurones, temporarily abolishing pain signal transmission from the application site; the 10.56% concentration provides powerful dermal anaesthesia suitable for procedures requiring mid-to-deep dermal access such as filler injections, laser resurfacing, and microneedling. The 30 g tube format is suited to individual procedure use or single-patient dispensing.
protocol: Apply a generous uniform layer (2–3 mm thick) to the clean, dry treatment area; cover with occlusive film and leave for 20–30 minutes. Remove excess cream with gauze and cleanse the area before the procedure. Maximum application: do not exceed 1.74 g cream (187 mg lidocaine) within 24 hours. Avoid mucous membranes and broken skin. Anaesthetic effect persists approximately 60–90 minutes after removal of the cream.

description: NEO-CAIN CREAM (500 g jar) is the professional bulk-size presentation of the 10.56% lidocaine topical anaesthetic, providing high-volume supply for multi-patient clinical settings or practices performing large treatment areas. The formulation delivers high-concentration sodium channel blockade for effective cutaneous anaesthesia to depths suitable for fractional mesotherapy (2–4 mm) and thread lifting procedures (5–8 mm) under adequate occlusion time. The 500 g professional jar minimises per-session cost for high-throughput aesthetic clinics.
protocol: Dispense from the jar with a spatula to the clean, dry treatment area; apply a uniform 2–3 mm thick layer and cover with occlusive film for 20–30 minutes (up to 45 minutes for deeper procedures). Remove and wipe clean before proceeding. Maximum dose per session: 1.74 g cream (187 mg lidocaine) on intact skin per 24 hours. Maintain aseptic technique when accessing the bulk container to prevent contamination.

description: NEO PRO CREAM is a eutectic lidocaine/prilocaine combination anaesthetic cream containing lidocaine 25 mg/g and prilocaine 25 mg/g in a 450 g professional bulk jar. The eutectic binary mixture penetrates intact skin more effectively than either anaesthetic alone, producing anaesthesia to 5 mm depth under occlusion by co-blocking voltage-gated sodium channels in A-delta and C sensory fibres; prilocaine carries a lower cardiovascular toxicity profile than lidocaine alone at equivalent doses. Indicated for needle injections, laser, thread lifting, and body procedures requiring deep dermal anaesthesia.
protocol: Apply a 2–3 mm thick layer of cream under occlusive wrap; allow 45–60 minutes for deep procedure anaesthesia (5–8 mm depth) or 30 minutes for superficial applications. Remove cream and cleanse the area before starting. Do not exceed 184 mg lidocaine per session on intact skin. Avoid use on mucosae, perianal skin, or open wounds without specialist indication. Prilocaine may cause methaemoglobinaemia at very high doses; observe standard dose limits.

description: J-CAIN is a high-strength lidocaine topical anaesthetic cream at 10.56% concentration, supplied in a 500 g professional jar for aesthetic clinics and tattoo studios requiring bulk numbing agent. Lidocaine at 10.56% achieves effective sodium channel blockade in epidermal and upper-dermal nerve endings within 15–25 minutes of occlusive application, providing temporary elimination of surface pain suitable for all categories of minimally invasive dermatological procedures. The large-format container supports multi-patient or high-volume single-session use.
protocol: Apply a thick, even coat of J-CAIN to the clean, dry skin area and cover with cling film or occlusive dressing for 20–30 minutes prior to the procedure; remove and wipe clean before starting. Maximum dose: 187 mg lidocaine (approximately 1.74 g cream) per 24 hours on intact skin. Do not apply to damaged skin, mucous membranes, or around the eyes. Anaesthetic effect lasts approximately 60–90 minutes post-removal.

description: SM CREAM (500 g jar) delivers lidocaine at 10.56% concentration in a professional bulk topical anaesthetic formulation for high-volume clinical use. The high-concentration lidocaine base rapidly penetrates the stratum corneum under occlusion to block sodium ion channels in superficial sensory nerve terminals, providing effective pre-procedural anaesthesia for laser treatments, microneedling, mesotherapy, semi-permanent make-up, and injection procedures. The 500 g format is economical for aesthetic clinics treating multiple patients per day.
protocol: Apply liberally (2–3 mm thick layer) to the clean treatment area, cover with occlusive film, and leave in place for 20–30 minutes prior to the procedure. Remove all excess cream with gauze before starting. Maximum dose: 1.74 g cream (187 mg lidocaine) per 24 hours on intact skin. Keep away from eyes and mucosal surfaces. Onset approximately 15–20 minutes; effect persists 60–90 minutes after removal.

description: SM CREAM (30 g tube) offers the same 10.56% lidocaine topical anaesthetic formulation as the bulk jar format in a compact, single-patient or portable 30 g tube. Lidocaine achieves rapid sodium channel blockade in cutaneous peripheral nociceptors under occlusion, effectively numbing the treatment zone within 15–20 minutes for procedures including filler injection, laser hair removal, tattooing, and microneedling. The tube format reduces cross-contamination risk compared to multi-patient bulk containers.
protocol: Squeeze a sufficient amount (2–3 mm layer) onto the treatment area, cover with occlusive wrap, and leave for 20–30 minutes; wipe clean before the procedure. Limit application to 1.74 g of cream (187 mg lidocaine) per 24 hours on intact skin. Do not apply to open wounds or mucous membranes. Anaesthetic effect lasts approximately 60–90 minutes; proceed promptly after removing the cream.

description: Muchcaine (100 g tube) is a eutectic lidocaine 25 mg/g and prilocaine 25 mg/g topical anaesthetic cream in a 100 g tube, providing an intermediate-size option between the 30 g personal tube and 500 g bulk jar. The EMLA-type eutectic formulation penetrates intact skin under occlusion to depths of 3–5 mm, blocking both A-delta mechanonociceptors and C-fibre polymodal nociceptors via sodium channel inhibition, reducing pain associated with injections, laser, and permanent cosmetics. The dual-agent formula provides broader sodium channel coverage with a favourable safety margin.
protocol: Apply a uniform 2–3 mm layer to the clean, dry area and cover with occlusive wrap for 30–45 minutes (45–60 minutes for deeper procedures). Remove and wipe the area clean before beginning the treatment. Do not exceed 184 mg lidocaine per 24 hours on intact skin. Prilocaine can cause methaemoglobinaemia at excessive doses; adhere to dose limits. Not for use on mucous membranes without specific clinical indication.

description: MUCHCAINE (500 g jar) is the professional bulk-size presentation of the 10.56% lidocaine topical anaesthetic, formulated for large-area treatment or multi-patient daily use in high-volume aesthetic clinics. The 10.56% lidocaine concentration blocks peripheral sodium channels in cutaneous nociceptors under occlusion, achieving effective surface-to-mid-dermal anaesthesia within 15–25 minutes — suitable for full-face laser, extensive tattooing, and large mesotherapy grids. The 500 g jar maximises cost efficiency for practices with high throughput.
protocol: Using a spatula (to maintain sterility), apply a 2–3 mm thick layer to the clean, dry treatment area; cover with occlusive film for 20–30 minutes, then wipe clean before the procedure. Maximum dose: 1.74 g cream (187 mg lidocaine) per 24 hours on intact skin. Avoid eyes and mucosal surfaces. Use a fresh spatula for each patient to prevent cross-contamination. Anaesthetic effect persists 60–90 minutes post-removal.

description: MUCHCAINE (30 g tube) is the individual-use or clinic-sample format of the 10.56% lidocaine topical anaesthetic cream, providing single-application or limited-session supply in a compact tube. Lidocaine at 10.56% concentration delivers rapid sodium channel blockade in superficial dermal sensory neurones under occlusion, achieving anaesthesia adequate for minimally invasive procedures including injections, laser, microneedling, and tattooing. The tube format supports single-patient dispensing and eliminates shared-container contamination risks.
protocol: Squeeze a generous layer (2–3 mm) onto the clean, dry treatment area and cover with occlusive film for 20–30 minutes; remove and wipe the area clean before the procedure. Maximum dose: 1.74 g cream (187 mg lidocaine) per 24 hours on intact skin. Do not apply to mucous membranes or broken skin. Onset within 15–20 minutes under occlusion; anaesthetic effect persists approximately 60–90 minutes after removal.

description: LIDOCAINE HCl Inj 2% is a sterile injectable solution of lidocaine hydrochloride at 20 mg/mL in a 20 mL vial, formulated for local infiltration anaesthesia, nerve block, and intravenous regional anaesthesia (Bier block) in clinical and aesthetic settings. Lidocaine HCl reversibly inhibits voltage-gated sodium channels in peripheral nerve axons, blocking both sensory and motor conduction in a dose-dependent, reversible manner to produce local anaesthesia lasting 60–120 minutes without vasoconstrictors. Each box contains 10 sterile vials for professional procedural use.
protocol: For local infiltration: inject 0.5–5 mL per site using a 25–30G needle, delivering a maximum of 4.5 mg/kg (without adrenaline) per session; aspirate before each injection to avoid intravascular placement. For facial aesthetic procedures, use 0.5–1 mL per block point at 27–30G with slow infiltration. Onset: 2–5 minutes; duration: 60–90 minutes. Do not exceed maximum safe dose; have resuscitation equipment available for nerve block procedures.

description: LAENNEC (50-ampoule box) is a pharmaceutical-grade human placenta hydrolysate developed by Japan Bio Products Co., Ltd., containing over 200 bioactive components including low-molecular-weight peptides, 18 amino acids, 14 growth factors (EGF, FGF, HGF, IGF), and 26 minerals. The hydrolysate exerts hepatoprotective effects through hepatocyte growth factor stimulation and oxidative stress reduction, while its growth factor profile promotes cellular regeneration, collagen synthesis, and immunomodulation — supporting both liver function restoration and non-surgical skin rejuvenation. Indicated for chronic hepatitis, liver fibrosis, atopic dermatitis, and aesthetic anti-ageing protocols.
protocol: Standard adult dose: 2 mL subcutaneous or intramuscular injection once daily (gluteus maximus or abdomen using a 26–30G needle), with frequency increased to 2–3 times daily for severe hepatic indications. For aesthetic mesotherapy protocols, administer 1–2 mL intradermal or subdermal once or twice weekly; a standard rejuvenation course is 10–20 sessions. Clinical hepatic reassessment including ALT/AST at 4 and 12 weeks.

description: LAENNEC (10-ampoule box) is the smaller clinical pack of the Japan Bio Products human placenta hydrolysate injection, containing the same pharmaceutical-grade formulation as the 50-ampoule presentation in a 10-session supply. Each 2 mL ampoule delivers over 200 bioactive components including growth factors (EGF, FGF, IGF, HGF), amino acid peptides, and trace minerals that promote hepatocellular regeneration, fibroblast activation, and immune modulation for both hepatic therapy and aesthetic skin rejuvenation. The 10-ampoule format is suited to initiating a patient course or lower-volume clinical use.
protocol: Administer 2 mL subcutaneously or intramuscularly once daily for hepatic and systemic protocols, or 1–2 mL by intradermal/subdermal injection once to twice weekly for aesthetic rejuvenation courses. Use a 26–30G needle with aspiration confirmation. A 10-ampoule box represents approximately 1–2 weeks of daily systemic therapy or 5–10 aesthetic sessions. Monitor liver enzymes at baseline and 4 weeks for hepatic indications.
